page: Microsoft Azure D32alds_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor based on the x86_64 architecture. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 3.7 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It includes 1890 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests and exceptional text generation speeds for small language models. Multi-threaded decompression and cryptographic operations also rank in the top quartile, while memory bandwidth and database operations perform within the average range.

With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D32alds_v6 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-intensive workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The primary tradeoff is the lower memory allocation per core, which may limit its suitability for large in-memory databases. However, the combination of dedicated AMD EPYC cores, fast local storage, and strong single-core performance makes it highly suitable for software compilation, image processing, web serving, and small-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32alds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance features a low-memory configuration of 64.0 GB RAM (2.0 GB per vCPU) and includes 1890 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression and cryptographic execution. Its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a cost-effective hardware profile for compute-heavy tasks that do not demand massive memory allocations. The server is well-suited for workloads such as software compilation, image processing, web serving, and localized machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32alds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for compute-heavy tasks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32alds_v6 is a 32-vCPU virtual machine with 64 GB RAM and 1890 GB local storage, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, fast local storage, and high-performance single-core execution for intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM and 1890 GB of local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and small-model LLM text generation
